Fear and Greed is at
34
(Fear)
and BTC is at
$65K
this morning. Yesterday's CPI print came in well below
expectations, sending BTC above $64K within minutes and snapping
eight straight weeks of ETF outflows.

The macro mood has shifted fast. Lower inflation reduces the
case for further rate hikes, softens the dollar, and improves the
conditions for ETF inflows to return. The regulatory pipeline is
also moving, with major stablecoin decisions coming from both
sides of the Atlantic.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

JPMorgan Just Cut Estimates for Circle and Coinbase

JPMorgan lowered earnings forecasts for Circle and Coinbase on
July 14 after a restructured revenue deal with Hyperliquid
significantly reduced Circle's share of USDC reserve income.

The bank described the arrangement as a prisoner's dilemma,
pushing the two partners to compete against each other on future
distribution deals. USDC's circulating supply has contracted by
roughly $7 billion since March.

How does Hyperliquid's new revenue structure affect Circle's
stablecoin income?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/3OsJDncHZXZCWsbQRZWq4A~~/AAQRxRA~/TBFNlYgnlhGbUaNZzQUIAz7wMBW4hn8YsCaSq5idOdSfLgDL0hm4LiVp3dFFkC1m7uksCpFXGai0RKNCKt39N2yIWPVeijNKx5TbzNgH-XKT6fwIBy8afD0cwcpzwf3laYKZc2F0BeWDHpkKhw4bgKISmDZ22kshGd2z3Cv-kWgCWmIy6d5DA26oQYh0ofYf-Nr5g1lkoq1uNj-BF-pjR6dSXzEdgui92ENccnVmss1HFKolAiqdtP_ym6gd

75959.subject: Hyperliquid Outgrows Crypto
75959.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

Here are the biggest stories in we3:

* **[Hyperliquid](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/builder-deployed-markets-overtake-crypto-on-hyperliquid)**['s builder-deployed stock and commodity markets overtake its native crypto volume](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/builder-deployed-markets-overtake-crypto-on-hyperliquid)

* **[Sen. Lummis](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/lummis-says-clarity-act-text-coming-in-next-few-days)**[ says CLARITY Act bill text is coming within days](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/lummis-says-clarity-act-text-coming-in-next-few-days)

* **[Coinbase](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/coinbase-matches-robinhood-s-7-yield-with-a-different-design)**[ matches Robinhood's 7% USDC yield with a different subsidy design](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/coinbase-matches-robinhood-s-7-yield-with-a-different-design)

* **[EthSystems](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethsystems-launches-privacy-tools-for-institutional-ethereum)**[ launches confidential-transaction tools for institutional Ethereum](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethsystems-launches-privacy-tools-for-institutional-ethereum)

* **[Tether](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/tether-leads-7m-series-a-in-pact-labs-for-usa-payroll)**[ leads a $7M round in Pact Labs to push USAT into payroll](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/tether-leads-7m-series-a-in-pact-labs-for-usa-payroll)

Hyperliquid was built to trade crypto perpetuals, and it settles more of them than anywhere else onchain. This month, most of the volume on the venue started flowing somewhere else.

**Builder-deployed markets** for stocks, commodities, and indices — the **HIP-3** markets any team can spin up on **Hyperliquid** — [overtook the exchange's native crypto volume](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/builder-deployed-markets-overtake-crypto-on-hyperliquid) on Tuesday, the latest in a run of weekday sessions this month where non-crypto contracts led. They still fade on weekends, and single-name stocks alone trail crypto.

The milestone is what the numbers imply about the venue. A crypto perps exchange is turning into a general onchain derivatives layer, and the growth is coming from markets its own users deployed. If that holds through weekends and beyond a few tickers, Hyperliquid stops being a crypto venue and becomes a trading venue that happens to run onchain.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

##### PARTNER

# **The Defiant Exclusive: Invest in High-Potential Startups Like These**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4b0ccb0c-4938-4401-999f-c9cf4d79b501/TIME_CircleKrakenMysten_large.png?t=1784068823)
Caption: 

**_The Defiant_**_ _and **Alumni Ventures** are teaming up this week only to give readers early [access to high-potential startup opportunities](https://www.av.vc/funds/blockchain/the-defiant-ad?utm_m

A decade of redefining money — and a personalized look back at your onchain life.

View in browser (https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMxg5m_5PW7Y9pgv6lZ3m1W5KkRMz2c9QyzW5TXV3j3hd4rJW6XL4dN1LHxslW7FFmv-817JlJW4yRVRN2YVz5MW74LFMH8mFbscW1Pc9ZB5nW-mnW7XBmqf7s395kW66cV4M6C3gdpW2DFvTh2j2ty6W6r-plW5LJVTMW3TJ27b2YvnJdW2HBcM86-cmWVW42P9Qn6-xzxJW48szQX4QWVVBW3q-f1q669g7bVTd-Nc8XHmrTW7YTb8c3MYFmXW1NHDZY5j5h13W50whk94fJfwPW8HLpx-2WM-ZYW1FdnhM7XzSP1W4Nz7q-18X-nwW3kZfQD67Hm3sW6nMd1s63MjDmN72y0HhZ424sW37hq5H5fNbVbW1L35M17Y0CJzW6s6ZCh4WWH_dW5Xy-Gr17Bhh0W1v2b848jdc6NW5rDXWb8PFTXmW4Rt_w05Y5Q8VW3WLJY17NNYhHW74Hy9L7dFKSVW27mRSs2khnpxW3xC6329hcbZwW4qzZ4z4G0cbmW7CbsnS5CC1x8W5gPXp73LxfMbW428GkQ6trj_VW73F1mn3c7pW5f6Vpmzs04 )

MetaMask (https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMwH3prCCW6N1vHY6lZ3l5W2VStbT8pXP50W63FKYK3ddMtFW72Qy6t5TrB-gW4R55pc13pwH-W4DHBkj26GYQqW64NrVm3mpDC8N8pcLvyN_XKvW8_Qy6p7GCB6MN3dlR6-_xhsRN5JgyZbXtDrPW5kb0xj1HbwkvW70bm8F4xTXSlN7gZWBywH6lwW3_Lz7H8ycwWdW6tjyzR1YCd4qV-6_J43kw0GnW2LWMyp2bPBQVN6BF3pt_0NyfW3x1RzD2l6-1tW79sYkX93VLTSW39Wts83jN_qWW1WnSTM2-NczPf5jF7bs04 )

A decade of MetaMask

On July 14, 2016, MetaMask went live: one small browser extension, built on the bet that people should be able to hold and use their money directly. 

Ten years later: Millions of downloads, used in nearly every country on Earth, trillions of dollars moved. Every transaction signed by someone like you. 

We didn't get here alone. Whether you joined in the ICO days, DeFi Summer, the NFT boom, or last week, this milestone is yours too. 

So we made a little something for you: a tour of your history onchain, alongside ours. Just open up MetaMask to try it out. (Best experienced on mobile.)

See your life onchain
(https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMwn3gP0xW69sMD-6lZ3m9W4QRvtF6fC7tTVLWPzm466gtvW40sL3Q8VzYV7W3bSCTg4zfzG3W97VglV4cj750W49MW8l7G7Qj5W5fRJDj24RBrWW6FKpst2sJZZ9W293pfv52JdWdW8kqkZK7b-kGZW5wxfRg8sWgb_W394SFb9fz4H5W8LVW_j10qxMgN2tkqnc5HpBzV43vrs8YxpZzW8hP1D_7PwYsnW2XPdT38swQ8HN36wR_D8ltlmW7GNfsB9dn3clW2zQDmZ3DKtc7f7JpZh204 )

(https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMwn5fkwKW69t95C6lZ3pDW5Qh5Pz2gMZVwN5G_GzsWrT13Vrsmlc4HNjyZW5v18B37hK5b9W7jQF-L5bhwc_W2D5x4P1hqd_QVM-4vB7PWjVYW63JMFP52Bj_cW6JM-tS9c9GGKW7v4TG21Vq5rMW1tDp-X5xBZ14W4tSMR-67rD0YW2p8BJr5w1tFlW6C-BHk6KGZ--VTc6pb5nHQq1W98PgHb8zpJ0lW2DZcnx5J2ZPzW1GRQyB7FY844W8YgKMt1VwFfkW8_ccRq36zHPPVWvzGN4ttHs5W62CX-h3BGPH9W5ZddKJ8vk3N3N2LVg3TR0pJ9W4Y2GzV6xJ_tSW4Nlx_h354VS1W1xSDHH8VSXMqW6t7qVM8PhkdpW2dFthY45GnJlW6zV6jY4BFS7KW5KRvs_8BV-GvW6DQSXp2bgrlHN63QBfpTMhhHW47xtRS19cV0HW8SJPKF1mDFKwN7wPPgb9GZrvf2HrXw204 )

75945.subject: Trump has no good options for reopening Hormuz
75945.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt24UplVtX3zjahkHQdaqO2g5-5JvaIRHMEbYkVsmo9zYvR824BUHtNiik880HeCSxqpcyFcWXtE10K26UofdRieBxkjSL5_VZMMJHTE 

July 14th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt25BAJa1bPXtogt41zQ_VERPTAqsGAATsSNLXPY1MjrzTwCnxlTBMfSBaw7_S8ogPovETtQ_AF0dKUzL4zbnU8nDKZj_bI9jfXuAIdM 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt26dK4EIBV4s3YKlnGqGvIy6K6wwzqzWKBM5Oi1oSSxWz3MdagOj4WbIKpNMIjM0QSNZ2f0lij5PKBY1CP2_By0kl2KutyWD9f2LGYc 


 


 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 Missiles and drones are flying in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz is closed once again. Oil prices have jumped. But neither Iran nor America is gaining an upper hand from the resumption of conflict and both are suffering (as is the rest of the world). Our analysis shows why the least-bad option for both sides is the truce they are 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt27eEs8zVENFUycFhTrvGhDNbGiT4Ye7_xRgiK91A1m3ci06vSAorxDTDn5Yhpq7D4cJk8WoEevQVuk2Ejqeoewm-SaCa1onR0 
shooting to bits .
 


 How do you ensure that artificial intelligence will benefit humanity rather than doom it? Sir Demis Hassabis, a co-founder of Google DeepMind, has an interesting plan. He lays it out
